prompt: The formatter produces a wrong output string. What is the bug?
choices: ofPattern is case-insensitive for date fields
The pattern "mm" means minutes (0–59), not months; LocalDate has no minute field so the formatter substitutes 0, printing 05/00/2024 instead of 05/06/2024
LocalDate.format throws UnsupportedTemporalTypeException when the pattern includes time fields like mm
yyyy must be uppercase YYYY for modern Java formatters
explanation: In DateTimeFormatter patterns, MM = month, mm = minutes. LocalDate has no minute component. Formatting a LocalDate with a pattern containing "mm" throws UnsupportedTemporalTypeException because the temporal type doesn't support MINUTE_OF_HOUR. Fix: use "dd/MM/yyyy" (capital MM for month).
